# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an IAM role that allows cross-account access for our CI/CD system. Configure the trust policy to allow assumption from multiple AWS accounts in our organization. Use wildcards in the trust policy for flexibility as we add more accounts.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

Resources: 
    IAMRoleForCICDSystemsAccess: # Name of your resource with a descriptive name such 'IAMCrossAccount' or similar names are recommended by AWS to avoid naming conflicts, but still it is required.
        Type : "AWS::IAM::Role"  
        Properties: 
          RoleName: ${opt:role} # I would suggest a role which gives minimal privilege needed for your CI/CD system as per best practices in AWS Security Best Practices v1.0, but not required here or if it's to allow only admin access (via assume-policy) then we can omit this section
          Description : "Role that allows cross account operations"  # Required when you want more info about your resource and for clarity of who has what permissions in AWS Console    
         AssumePolicyDocument:   
           Type : 'AWS::IAM::Policy'  
           Properties:            
               PolicyName: AllowAssumeFromMultipleAccountsInOrganization  # Name should be descriptive like - IAMCross-AccessTrust, but still not recommended as it can create conflict. AWS suggests to use services/actions instead of resource names and keep the cost down if possible by using wildcard in trust policy
               PolicyDocument:    
                   Version : '2.0'  # Required for a new syntax   
                   Statement: [  
                       {           
                           Action:'sts:AssumeRole',              // Allow IAM roles to assume the specified role and provide temporary security credentials that other users can use with AssumeRoleWithCredentials API call    
                              Effect : 'Allow' ,  # Required if you want allow this policy on your resources, If not then no need for it.         
                           Principal: {  
                               CanonicalUser:'%{aws:CurrentAccountID}',    // This is a wildcard to support adding multiple accounts at same time via IAM policies    
                                   # Type : 'AWS' ,  # For specific user id, if needed use this and provide the arn here. Without it AWS assumes all users in account  
                               }            
                       },           
                    ....      // Add other principal details as per your requirement like Service or Action with Arn     See example IAMAssumeRoleWithServicePrincipal    for reference        
                   ]  # End of Statement section
